title: activity — product analytics events
description: Product analytics events (Segment / Mixpanel / PostHog). Distinct from `events.json` (server-side pub/sub) and `metrics.json` (ops telemetry). Every event carries a `consent_tier` that gates delivery through the analytics pipeline against the caller's consent state. Activity events are primarily `functional` (help us understand feed usage) with two `essential` events (feed adoption baseline).

events.name: activity_recorded
events.description: Sampled at 5% — activity capture is high-volume. Feeds capacity + usage-pattern analysis.

events.name: activity_feed_opened
events.description: Adoption baseline — how often do users open the feed? Essential tier because it's necessary product-usage signal.

events.name: activity_feed_subscribed
events.description: Sampled at 10% — feed subscribes happen at every page load.

events.name: activity_row_clicked
events.description: Engagement funnel — which activity rows get clicked, which are ignored. Powers 'the feed is noisy on log_name X' insights.

events.name: activity_feed_filter_applied
events.description: Which filters do users apply? Informs which filter surfaces to prioritise.

events.name: activity_export_requested
events.description: Adoption of the export feature. Essential tier because it's a paid entitlement — track conversion to inform Medium+ upsell.

events.name: activity_digest_opened
events.firesAt: Digest email link click (tracked via UTM)
events.description: How often are recipients engaging with digest emails? Informs whether the digest is delivering value.

events.name: activity_digest_opted_in
events.description: Digest adoption. Essential — paid entitlement conversion.

events.name: activity_purge_completed
events.description: Fed to operational analytics (not customer-facing). Aggregated at the platform level.
